title: Consistency Models (clickable map)
author: Kyle Kingsbury (Jepsen)
note: A rigorous map of how isolation and consistency models relate, showing exactly where read committed, snapshot isolation, and serializability sit relative to each other.

title: Transactions: Myths, Surprises and Opportunities
author: Martin Kleppmann
note: Kleppmann's 41-minute Strange Loop talk (video + slides) covering what ACID really means, weak-isolation race conditions, and modern serializable algorithms — a direct companion to this chapter.

title: CMU 15-445 Lecture 17: Two-Phase Locking (Fall 2024)
author: Andy Pavlo (Carnegie Mellon University)
note: Lecture notes walking through 2PL's growing/shrinking phases, strict 2PL, deadlock detection and prevention, and lock hierarchies — the textbook mechanics behind serializability via locking.

title: Serializable Isolation for Snapshot Databases
author: Michael J. Cahill, Uwe Rohm, Alan D. Fekete
note: The SIGMOD 2008 paper that introduced SSI: detect dangerous pairs of rw-antidependency edges at runtime and abort one transaction, getting serializability without 2PL's blocking.

title: Serializable Snapshot Isolation in PostgreSQL
author: Dan R. K. Ports, Kevin Grittner
note: VLDB 2012 account of making SSI production-ready in PostgreSQL 9.1, including how predicate locks handle phantoms and how safe retry works after serialization failures.

title: PostgreSQL Documentation: Transaction Isolation
author: PostgreSQL Global Development Group
note: Official reference for how a real MVCC database maps the SQL-standard levels: read committed per-statement snapshots, repeatable read as snapshot isolation, and serializable via SSI.

title: Hermitage: Testing Transaction Isolation Levels
author: Martin Kleppmann
note: Kleppmann's runnable test suite (built as research for DDIA) showing exactly which anomalies — dirty reads, lost updates, write skew, phantoms — each database's isolation levels actually prevent.

title: Two-Phase Commit (Patterns of Distributed Systems)
author: Unmesh Joshi (martinfowler.com)
note: Step-by-step walkthrough of 2PC's prepare and commit phases with diagrams and code, including why participants must log decisions durably to survive coordinator crashes.
